solutions.solution:
(D) By applying the above decoding method for (P @ Q $ M # T), we draw the following family diagram

Married couple D♀ ←→ F♂     Married couple P♂ ←→ Q♀

Daughter R♀ connects to Mother H, where P is the paternal grandfather of T, and R is the sister of H. Mother connects to Father M♂, then to T.

Hence, P is the paternal grandfather of T.

(C) By applying above decoding method, we can check all the options for the required relationship
(a) (R $ D @ F # H) = Rejected

R♂ connects to Mother, then D♂ Married couple ←→ F♂

Now, F cannot be related with H as according to given condition for F # H, F must be a male.
(b) (H % D @ F $ R) = Rejected

Married couple D♀ ←→ F♂, with Daughter connecting to Mother H♀, then to R

Here, gender of R is not known. So, this option is also rejected.
(c) (R % D @ F $ H) = Selected

Married couple R♂ ←→ F♂, with Daughter connecting to Mother, then Sister connecting to H

Hence, no need to check other options.

(D) By applying above decoding method for (F @ D % K # H), we draw the following family diagram

K♂ connects as Daughter to Married couple F♂ ←→ D♀, with Sister H connecting.

F is the brother-in-law of H.

Hence, F the brother-in-law of H.

(C) By applying above decoding method, we can check all the options for the required relationship
(a) (N % F @ D $ H) = Rejected

Married couple F♀ ←→ D♂, with Daughter N♀ connecting to Mother H

Here, gender of H is not known. So, cannot be determined
(b) (N % F @ D % H) = Rejected

H connects as Daughter, with Married couple F♀ ←→ D♂, then Daughter N♀

Here, gender of H is not known and H can be either grandfather or grandmother of N.
(c) (N % F @ D $ H # R) = Selected

Married couple F♀ ←→ D♂, with Daughter N♀ connecting to Mother H♀, then Father R

H is the brother of N

Therefore, there is no need to check other options because we have already obtained the answer.

(C) By applying above decoding method, we can check all the options for the required relationship
(a) (H + J + T)

H♀ connects as Father to J♂, then Father to T, where H is the paternal grandfather of T

Hence, there is no need to check other options, as we have already obtained the answer.
